message:
Dynamic builder: write-side parity with codegen for primitives + text/data

defineSchema gains an optional second argument carrying the struct's
wire-format dimensions:

  defineSchema(spec, { dataWords, ptrWords })

Schemas without dimensions stay read-only — buildDynamic refuses them
with a clear message pointing back at defineSchema.

buildDynamic(cpp, schema) returns a single-shot builder. .set(name, val)
dispatches by field type to either a direct memory write (primitives,
floats, bool packed into bits) or the cpp_any_builder_set_text/data
boundary call for pointer-typed fields. .finalize() returns framed
bytes, wire-compatible with codegen — round-trip tests verify openDynamic
sees the same values that went in.

Caught one BigInt-vs-Number wasm-boundary mismatch along the way:
cpp_any_int64_at takes (i32 byte_offset, i64 default), but the reader
was passing BigInt(off) for the first arg, so reads of int64/uint64
fields threw "Cannot convert a BigInt value to a number" through
node:wasm. Fixed.

Coverage today:
  read  ✓ primitives ✓ lists-of-primitives ✓ nested struct ✓ list-of-struct
  write ✓ primitives ✓ text ✓ data — lists + nested struct still codegen
        only (would need wasm-side list/struct builder support)

7 new tests bring the total to 146 (was 139).

files.patch:
@@ -191,7 +191,27 @@ reader.pick(["name", "age"]);   // one wasm round trip, only the fields you ask
 reader.fields.email;            // Proxy access for ergonomic single-field reads
 ```
 
-The descriptor is wire-compatible with what `npx capnwasm gen` emits (`SomeReader._FIELDS`). A build step that strips a generated reader to its `_FIELDS` object can feed it directly to `openDynamic`. Supported field kinds: `text`, `data`, `uint8/16/32`, `int8/16/32`, `int64`, `uint64`, `float32/64`, `bool`, plus `listUint8/16/32/64`, `listInt8/16/32/64`, `listFloat32/64`, `listBool`, `listText`, `listData`, plus `{ kind: "struct", slot, schema }` for nested structs and `{ kind: "listStruct", slot, element }` for lists of structs. Reads cover everything the codegen path does; the dynamic reader is read-only — `buildSomething` builders are still codegen.
+The descriptor is wire-compatible with what `npx capnwasm gen` emits (`SomeReader._FIELDS`). A build step that strips a generated reader to its `_FIELDS` object can feed it directly to `openDynamic`. Supported field kinds: `text`, `data`, `uint8/16/32`, `int8/16/32`, `int64`, `uint64`, `float32/64`, `bool`, plus `listUint8/16/32/64`, `listInt8/16/32/64`, `listFloat32/64`, `listBool`, `listText`, `listData`, plus `{ kind: "struct", slot, schema }` for nested structs and `{ kind: "listStruct", slot, element }` for lists of structs.
+
+For the write side, pass the struct's wire-format dimensions and use `buildDynamic`:
+
+```js
+import { defineSchema, buildDynamic } from "capnwasm/dynamic";
+
+const User = defineSchema({
+  id:     { kind: "uint64", offset: 0 },
+  active: { kind: "bool",   bitOffset: 64 },
+  name:   { kind: "text",   slot: 0 },
+}, { dataWords: 2, ptrWords: 1 });
+
+const b = buildDynamic(cpp, User);
+b.set("id", 42);
+b.set("active", true);
+b.set("name", "Alice");
+const bytes = b.finalize();   // framed Cap'n Proto bytes, wire-compatible with codegen
+```
+
+Builders cover primitives + text + data. Lists and nested-struct builders aren't in this pass — codegen still wins for those write paths.
 
 When to choose dynamic:

@@ -71,6 +71,19 @@ The dynamic reader now covers everything the codegen path does on the
 read side: primitives, lists of primitives, nested structs, lists of
 structs. No remaining capability gaps for reads.
 
+**Update (2026-04-30, fifth pass):** dynamic builder landed. Pass
+`{ dataWords, ptrWords }` as a second argument to `defineSchema`, then
+use `buildDynamic(cpp, schema)` to build messages at runtime:
+
+    const b = buildDynamic(cpp, schema);
+    b.set("name", "Alice"); b.set("age", 36);
+    const bytes = b.finalize();
+
+Covers primitives + text + data. Lists and nested-struct write paths
+aren't in this pass — the wasm side doesn't expose a builder for those
+yet (codegen handles them via direct memory writes off `data_ptr` plus
+hand-rolled list/struct pointer encoding).
+
 ## 3. RPC pipelining is implemented but not pipelined under `await`
 
 The implementation handles `r.cap.call(...)` chained on an unresolved question
